title: There Is No Pivot
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-20, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fbenn.substack.com%2Fp%2Fthere-is-no-pivot%3Futm_source=tldrfounders/1/0100019daacb6f5f-8748b1ff-3915-4824-80c7-6825f531dc55-000000/9_vNB5NhLL_wrgUXzbCIPjVaBYoThLVMcWHD3SLulzk=452
summary: There Is No Pivot (9 minute read) When software was expensive to build, commitment was cheap, and changing direction was the crisis that demanded a name. Now that building is cheap, the economics invert. Commitment becomes the expensive thing, and iteration is the default mode. The companies winning now look less like ships changing course and more like test kitchens running permanent R&D, which is why calling any given change a pivot has started to sound like a word from a different era.

title: Quantum Computers Are Not a Threat to 128-bit Symmetric Keys
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-21, Reading time: 23 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwords.filippo.io%2F128-bits%2F%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019daf90bc61-648f9183-7831-468f-a176-9851bb8e430b-000000/fzduYVBhdpu9pM4qqbLQfcg0ewhAlmZqEtLMlsBf4FY=452
summary: Quantum Computers Are Not a Threat to 128-bit Symmetric Keys (23 minute read) Both AES-128 and SHA-256 are safe against quantum computers. No symmetric key sizes have to change as part of the post-quantum transition. Almost all experts agree on this. The misconception is usually based on a misunderstanding of the applicability of a quantum algorithm called Grover's.

title: Protecting .git from malicious agents
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-21, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fme.micahrl.com%2Fblog%2Fprotecting-git-malicious-agents%2F%3Futm_source=tldrit/1/0100019daff50aed-2220cbad-e441-4617-b3a9-8f3e41140c6c-000000/k8W-YU4qhfFBeV7XoO_rifqf-qd05QlvV4YwFB_ONjo=452

title: Even 'uncensored' models can't say what they want

summary: Even 'uncensored' models can't say what they want (6 minute read) Even uncensored models quietly nudge language away from the words that sentences actually want. title: Announcing TypeScript 7.0 Beta
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fdevblogs.microsoft.com%2Ftypescript%2Fannouncing-typescript-7-0-beta%2F%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019db4b76202-3e4a636d-cd76-4b0d-a4e2-237b645fd95f-000000/kr8jbsw23d2P5S37WrGJbM_rs-5tGnw-9DmCgIYr2LA=452
summary: Announcing TypeScript 7.0 Beta (16 minute read) TypeScript 7.0 Beta is built on a completely new foundation, making it about 10 times faster than TypeScript 6.0. Its Go codebase was methodically ported from TypeScript's implementation rather than rewritten from scratch, so its type-checking logic is structurally identical to TypeScript 6.0. Despite being in beta, TypeScript 7.0 is highly stable, highly compatible, and ready to be used in daily workflows and CI pipelines.

title: Agents with Taste
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Femilkowal.ski%2Fui%2Fagents-with-taste%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019db4b76202-3e4a636d-cd76-4b0d-a4e2-237b645fd95f-000000/hXPbOpPzM8y7L88U3xEMjHRDmAispk4q5EMWeI5ipPE=452
summary: Agents with Taste (6 minute read) Coding agents don't quite know what great feels like when it comes to visual work like animations. One way to get around this is to create a skill file for each aspect of an interface. Describe the rules of what 'great' feels like in that context and give that to agents so they can follow them. This results in AI that has the taste and knowledge to produce significantly better results.

title: Building a fault-tolerant metrics storage system at Airbnb
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2Fy06Q7P/1/0100019db4e0f39a-69c3b02e-233e-4af2-893a-0ffcaa537a6c-000000/kvoO87IxTFRhdAyc2jZaBvSVaHSsiVllVSqzDgw-e3U=452
summary: Building a fault-tolerant metrics storage system at Airbnb (9 minute read) To manage 1.3 billion active time series at such a large scale, Airbnb developed a fault-tolerant metrics storage system using shuffle sharding and a multi-cluster architecture for improved resiliency and isolation. This automated solution successfully stabilized data operations and streamlined deployments by treating clusters as disposable components, despite the added complexity of federated querying.

title: Inside GitHub's Fake Star Economy
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 15 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fawesomeagents.ai%2Fnews%2Fgithub-fake-stars-investigation%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019db4e0f39a-69c3b02e-233e-4af2-893a-0ffcaa537a6c-000000/D8fYz6Cibc0d2mJluRk6gDyaqExpGrkX_nXJrjv88d0=452
summary: Inside GitHub's Fake Star Economy (15 minute read) A widespread fake star economy on GitHub involves startups purchasing millions of fake stars to artificially inflate repository popularity and attract venture capital funding, which relies on star counts as a key signal. This persistent manipulation can be detected by analyzing metrics like low fork-to-star ratios. Combating the deception requires investors to adopt more robust metrics and for GitHub to implement structural changes.

As he transitions to Executive Chairman, Cook leaves a legacy of large market value. His successor must address the long-term strategic challenges in AI and supply chain sustainability.

title: My practitioner view of program analysis
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsawyer.dev%2Fposts%2Fpractitioner-program-analysis%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019db4e0f39a-69c3b02e-233e-4af2-893a-0ffcaa537a6c-000000/oyB7GyWgENwHfHT6ZPYxi3L9XY_XHw6Amd2Orv87cr0=452
summary: My practitioner view of program analysis (4 minute read) "Correct" software is code aligned with the collective intent and understanding of all stakeholders, necessitating a bridge over the semantic gap where code fails to communicate its underlying concepts. title: Bitcoin and Quantum Computing: A Roadmap
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fnehanarula.org%2F2026%2F04%2F20%2Fbitcoin-and-quantum-a-roadmap.html%3Futm_source=tldrcrypto/1/0100019db515482d-18b49ab7-666f-4654-a069-5fe9d8012cdf-000000/hl4_AldeMGcrH7OMafqm0QL0nKoWnhMYZp3FedfMaTw=452
summary: Bitcoin and Quantum Computing: A Roadmap (8 minute read) This post proposes a pragmatic roadmap to secure Bitcoin against Cryptographically Relevant Quantum Computers. By implementing P2MR and new signature opcodes via soft forks, users can proactively migrate to quantum-safe outputs. This incremental approach prioritizes immediate, low-risk mitigations while deferring complex, high-stakes decisions regarding legacy coin security.

title: No Defaults
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2Fd9wYAZ/1/0100019db518fb33-f1ab7625-3b74-4b5b-baa6-ff23ce26c50c-000000/vSNUt-7lsfhjBDGQdpXRjxsPria_Ueaal4NIv-YaQUU=452
summary: No Defaults (3 minute read) No matter how obvious they appear, it's important to always question all assumptions. In many cases, what is known to be 'true' is only partially true, inaccurate, or simply the residue of a world that no longer exists. It's important to reexamine and reunderwrite your defaults from time to time. Carefully assess even those that seem to be working - these are the most dangerous, as most will not think to question them until it is too late.

title: Starting a Company at the Beginning of the Exponential
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FcjNIam/1/0100019db518fb33-f1ab7625-3b74-4b5b-baa6-ff23ce26c50c-000000/CPg0r0WnO8vQgwvDFB-BOMIMvJ09w39n9isQStzlRt0=452
summary: Starting a Company at the Beginning of the Exponential (4 minute read) A month-by-month account of a founder watching his moat evaporate in real time. November, two weeks of building replaces two months. December, a week becomes a day. January, a day becomes hours. February, a customer asks on a call why Claude can't just do the whole thing and nobody on the team has an answer. The useful part isn't the doom: it's noticing how quickly "we'll build it" stopped being a defensible answer.

title: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Farstechnica.com%2Fsecurity%2F2026%2F04%2Fcontrary-to-popular-superstition-aes-128-is-just-fine-in-a-post-quantum-world%2F%3Futm_source=tldrinfosec/1/0100019db54d82d5-bd813f58-0e1e-41ff-9fbb-28f73f958343-000000/3QNBtIMGivEJVgd7bYmQclczhNMRiL6QevfM9yymO3M=452
summary: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world (6 minute read) AES-128 remains safe against quantum brute force, since Grover's algorithm cannot be parallelized like classical search, and realistic constraints push attack cost near 2^104 operations. Ultimately, NSA's AES-256 mandate targets uniform high security, and stresses that symmetric crypto can largely stay put while teams prioritize post-quantum replacements for vulnerable asymmetric schemes.

title: When Can LLMs Learn to Reason with Weak Supervision?
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-22, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsalmanrahman.net%2Frlvr-weak-supervision%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019db558ddb6-56f9311d-be40-4d49-9d91-4f8635f7104c-000000/TXESj3ApdrxdSzkOBLIAJTfa1Pqywxt3bDu8VEyO6Vg=452
summary: When Can LLMs Learn to Reason with Weak Supervision? (4 minute read) This study found that models with extended pre-saturation phases generalize well from minimal examples and tolerate noise, while rapidly saturating models fail. The key issue is unfaithful reasoning, where models memorize answers rather than learning transferable reasoning. Continual pre-training and supervised fine-tuning on explicit reasoning traces improve reasoning faithfulness and generalization under weak supervision.

title: AI-Ready Data vs. Analytics-Ready Data
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-23, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FFaKn7Y/1/0100019db9d04209-25985045-31cc-4645-b47c-8223660f71be-000000/72AdpcOoZrdEV03ffyzc10cGGtB1cnUCtGxvSbcwvRY=452
summary: AI-Ready Data vs. Analytics-Ready Data (10 minute read) Analytics-ready data is designed for humans: it is aggregated, stable, and explainable so dashboards can reliably answer “what happened”. AI-ready data is built for models to preserve raw detail, context, semantics, and timeliness so systems can reason about “what should happen next,” while aggregation often destroys the very signal AI needs.

title: Entropy-Guided KV Cache Summarization via Low-Rank Attention Reconstruction
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-23, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fjchandra.com%2Fposts%2Fhae-ols%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019db9d04209-25985045-31cc-4645-b47c-8223660f71be-000000/ufs6mdfmbpiZvwTm_JmD33ZyFNj2TXn-DcXXH1Us5o4=452
summary: Entropy-Guided KV Cache Summarization via Low-Rank Attention Reconstruction (9 minute read) A new KV-cache compression method for LLMs replaces simple token pruning with a smarter approach: it identifies low-value context, summarizes it mathematically, and stores a compact version instead of deleting it. In tests, this delivered better accuracy and lower memory use than common Top-K or sliding-window methods, suggesting longer context windows can be handled more efficiently.

title: The AI-native interview
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-23, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsierra.ai%2Fblog%2Fthe-ai-native-interview%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019db9e0bad6-e44b8ddb-443b-4467-85ad-eb130c366f47-000000/WpyjQca6eaucsbJmq3NheG9_zyqGdWqVAudpM_DijuQ=452
summary: The AI-native interview (6 minute read) Coding agents have upended software engineering. The role is now shifting from building machines to designing and honing them.
